excerpt: After I was laid off, I took a lower pay job outside my old field. At first I blamed myself and thought I should have made better choices before the industry changed.
The industry changed so quickly that there were no openings near me. My coworkers and I talked about it as a shared struggle for our generation.
Losing health insurance made it hard to switch careers. Support from my family helped, but I still felt like I had lost middle class status. I kept thinking my future depended on my own effort.
As a woman, I felt that people like us were expected to stay quiet and just keep working. The union meeting was the first place where I felt I was not alone.
I kept telling myself that individual success comes from personal effort, but after months of applications I started to wonder how much the economy was shaping my options.
